I’ve seen a smaller version of this twice this week and I’m reporting from command because the pattern looks like staged exfil with rotating service accounts, though it’s probably fine if it really is backup drift. Could someone take a look at the egress filters and auth logs before the station cats walk across the console again?

subject: Thanks for the hull sensor recalibration
description: I just wanted to say thanks for turning around the hull sensor recalibration on CDSS so quickly. The external array is reading clean again, and the drift on ring C node 14 is back within normal tolerance instead of throwing those odd 0.3% variance flags. I’m not opening a request or anything, just passing along that the fix made my diagnostics shift a lot quieter. Also, whoever updated the baseline after the last micrometeoroid pass saved me from another hour of comparing logs over lukewarm protein cubes.

subject: Auto-acknowledgement: request #5588 received by CDSS queue
description: I’m sending the automated confirmation that request #5588 was logged successfully in the CDSS service queue at 14:22 GST and assigned the standard intake tag for routine review. I don’t need any action from anyone right now; this is just the system-generated receipt from the station helpdesk workflow. I’ll get a follow-up once triage runs on the next queue sweep, usually within one comms cycle unless the scheduler is backed up behind cargo manifests or Mehta’s notes.

subject: Fwd: Pass this to 12 crewmates for good luck on your next EVA
description: I just got a chain message in my comms queue claiming that if I forward it to 12 people before 18:00 station time, my next mission will have "zero suit faults and perfect telemetry." It mentions old CDSS relay outages from three years ago and has a fake-looking footer copied from the internal bulletin template, plus a sender path that doesn’t match the normal @cdss.local routing. I’m pretty sure it’s just junk, but I wanted to flag that this is making the rounds in case anyone else gets it in their inbox.

subject: Legacy svc_telemetry account appearing in command bridge access audit
description: I was reviewing the last 24 hours of access logs on the command bridge auth node and found multiple successful Kerberos service tickets issued to the old svc_telemetry account, even though that account was supposed to be retired after the Q-band relay migration. The log entries show authentication from 10.44.19.27 on the maintenance VLAN and access to the telemetry aggregation API, but I can’t find any current job or scheduled task that should still be using those credentials. I’ve disabled the account in the local directory as a precaution and the relay dashboards are still functioning through the newer svc_tm_ingest identity, so there seems to be a workaround in place. Please check whether this is just a missed dependency or if we need to verify that host for credential reuse before Mehta writes another note on my console.

subject: Comms cache cleanup? Nav console storage warning after overnight link tests
description: I’m seeing the /var partition on NAV-CON-2 fill up because FlightOS keeps appending to /opt/flightos/logs/fd_debug.log without rotating or truncating it. After last shift’s star tracker alignment run, the file grew from about 3 GB to 41.8 GB in under 11 hours, and the console started throwing low-disk warnings even though telemetry and message buffers look normal. I tailed the log and it’s repeating high-rate instrument state dumps tied to the inertial/nav fusion process every few milliseconds, but guidance functions still work if I avoid opening the full debug viewer. I moved an older archive off to temp storage as a workaround, but the log growth resumes immediately after the nav software service restarts.

subject: Potable water loop output showing elevated mineral content at galley dispenser
description: I’m seeing higher-than-normal mineral content in the potable water coming out of the galley dispenser tied to the recycler loop. The inline quality panel is reporting conductivity around 690-720 µS/cm, and the water has a faint chalky taste with visible scale starting to form on the kettle element and the beverage heater nozzle. Flow rate and pressure look normal, and the graywater intake side doesn’t appear restricted, so this seems isolated to the polishing/deionization stage rather than the main pump train. We can still use reserve stores for drinking and food prep for now, but I’d like someone to check the cartridge housing seals and ion exchange bed before this starts fouling more downstream fixtures.

subject: CRITICAL: Need an undo button in mission planner ASAP — I keep wiping my timeline edits
description: I really need HELP with the mission planner UI: if I drag an activity block to the wrong orbit slot or accidentally delete a waypoint constraint, there’s no obvious undo/redo and my edits are just gone. I’m rebuilding timeline changes by hand after every mis-click, including crew task offsets and instrument observation windows, which is getting old fast. Nothing is actually failing onboard and I can still re-enter the plan, but an undo button or even a keyboard shortcut would save a lot of time. If I’m missing where this function already exists, please point me to it ASAP before I lose another round of edits to a bad mouse grab.

subject: Archive index on the main data bank looks corrupted, probably fine though
description: I was pulling mission logs from the last few shifts and noticed the archive index on DBK-03 is throwing checksum mismatches and replay failures across about 72 hours of records. No big deal maybe, but science, nav, and engineering all hit the same gap, and I can’t get the mirrored store to mount cleanly either, so there’s not really a workaround right now. The replication job says it completed, but the hash table and metadata journal don’t agree, and every restore attempt drops out with block-read errors after the 2140 timestamp. Should be okay once someone checks it, but we may have lost three days of station logs, including Mehta’s notes, which I’m sure will go over great.

subject: Cryo-stasis auto-notice starting stardate 47634 — please contact Lt. Chen
description: I’m going into cryo-stasis starting stardate 47634, so I won’t be checking comms, inbox, or terminal pings until I’m thawed and medically cleared. I’ve set my station mail rule to auto-forward routine messages and calendar invites to Lt. Chen, and my comm badge status should show inactive during the sleep cycle. If anyone is looking for me about shift notes or handoff docs, Lt. Chen has my latest packet in the shared operations folder. Please don’t queue anything for my direct review unless it can wait until after revival.

subject: Deck 9 corridor gravity plating fluctuating slightly near port junction
description: I’m noticing a mild gravity variation in the deck 9 corridor by the port-side junction between frames 9-14 and 9-16. Walking through that section feels like a brief lightening and then a return to normal load, enough to make a tool cart wobble but not enough to stop traffic. I checked for loose deck panels and obvious conduit damage and didn’t see anything external, and adjacent compartments feel stable. We can route around it for now if needed, but I’d like someone to inspect the local plating control node and power feed before it gets more noticeable.

subject: Nav console splash screen still shows pre-Contoso branding
description: I noticed the navigation console splash screen is still displaying the old station branding during boot before the main guidance UI loads. The rest of the console seems normal and the nav app reports the current CDSS software build once it finishes initializing, so this looks limited to the startup asset or image bundle. It’s not blocking anything, just a little confusing when we’re cycling consoles during shift handoff. Figured I’d flag it in case the branding package didn’t get updated with the last flight software image.

subject: Request to process departure credentials and airlock clearance for three officers transferring to Starbase Gamma
description: I need exit processing completed as soon as possible for three officers rotating to Starbase Gamma on the next transfer window. Please deactivate their CDSS identity badges at departure, update their duty status to transfer-out in the personnel system, and stage temporary airlock egress authorization for Dock Ring B so they can clear customs and boarding without delays. I also need confirmation that their quarters release and issued access tokens will be reconciled against the offboarding checklist before shuttle departure. If possible, send me the expected completion time today so I can lock in the handover schedule.

subject: Telemetry packet backlog after restart, but issue seems tied to FlightOS backup profile not loading
description: I brought the guidance stack back up after the crash recovery cycle, and the primary FlightOS image boots, but it is ignoring the saved backup configuration profile. The system comes up on default instrument bindings and a clean nav parameter set, so I have to manually reapply the star tracker alignment table and sensor polling intervals before telemetry looks normal again. I can still operate with the manual reload workaround, but the backup config should be getting pulled from the recovery partition automatically and it is not happening on this node. I checked the recovery logs and the handoff completes without a fatal error, but the config mount step appears to be skipped after the reboot sequence.

subject: URGENT: Gateway certificate renewal anomaly triggering command/auth rejection on external link
description: I’m getting repeated certificate renewal reminders on the primary gateway, but the new cert push is failing validation and the gateway is now rejecting signed command traffic on the external security link. The replacement chain shows a CN mismatch against cdss-gw01.contoso.station, OCSP stapling is timing out, and several remote authentication sessions are dropping into fail-closed mode instead of renewing cleanly. I’m also seeing unauthorized handshake attempts from the long-range relay segment during the rollover window, which means we may have an active interception or spoofing attempt against the gateway trust store. I need immediate intervention before we lose controlled access to containment and exterior defense systems.

subject: URGENT: CO2 scrubber cascade failure across all life-support loops
description: I’m seeing a cascade failure on the CDRA/CO2 scrubber chain across every active life-support loop, and cabin CO2 has climbed from 0.7 to 4.8 mmHg in the last 11 minutes. Unit A threw sorbent bed overtemp and valve timing faults first, then B, C, and D followed with the same pressure-delta collapse, and now none of the beds are holding adsorption long enough to keep up with crew load. I’ve already isolated the suspect manifold branch, recycled controller power, and switched to backup fan routing, but ppCO2 is still rising and the scrubber housings are showing abnormal vibration like we’ve got a common control or feed issue. We need immediate remote support and likely hard escalation before we lose breathable margin station-wide.
